text.title: HN Culture, Audience, and Post Formats
text.summary: What Hacker News values and penalizes; audience profile; post type selection (Show HN, Launch HN, blog post, OSS); title formula with good and bad examples.

rule.statement: Write titles that describe what the product does technically, not what it achieves for the user. Avoid superlatives, buzzwords, and marketing adjectives entirely.
rule.rationale: HN's community actively downvotes and flags marketing language. The algorithm treats flag velocity as a quality signal. A flagged post disappears from the front page within minutes.

section.title: Audience Profile
section.p: HN readers are: software engineers and engineering managers (60%+), startup founders with technical backgrounds, investors affiliated with YC ecosystem, students and researchers. They are skeptical of hype, reward intellectual honesty, and test claims immediately. A broken demo or incorrect technical claim will be surfaced in comments within 15 minutes.

list.item: Show HN: [Product] — [Description] — for products with a public demo. No signup required to see value.
Launch HN: [Company] ([URL]) — for YC companies at batch launch. YC affiliation adds credibility automatically.
Technical blog post — link to a deep technical article. Title is the article headline, no "Show HN" prefix.
Open source project — Show HN with GitHub link. OSS projects get strong organic upvotes from the developer community.

rule.statement: Format: "Show HN: [What it does] — [How it's technically interesting or different]". State the fact; do not interpret it as valuable.
rule.rationale: The community decides whether it's valuable. Claiming value in the title reads as marketing and reduces trust.

example.code: Show HN: A terminal-based Git client written in Rust Show HN: We built a search engine for research papers Show HN: Open source alternative to Airtable
Show HN: The best way to manage your tasks (marketing claim) Show HN: Check out our startup! (vague, self-promotional) Show HN: Revolutionary AI-powered productivity app (hyperbolic)
